reasoning: Query explicitly asks for accommodation under ¥5,000/night in Kyoto. Response returns a hotel list with no price data at all — no price_band, no rate indication. The server cannot filter by price, but should at minimum surface hostels/guesthouses. Response lists luxury hotels (DoubleTree by Hilton) making it practically useless for a budget traveler.
missing_data: Price band fields for hotels; hostel and guesthouse type entries should appear first; budget accommodation categories (capsule hotels, hostels) are absent from top results.
improvement_hint: Add price_band field to hotel records; rank hostel/guesthouse lodging_type entries higher when budget constraint is implied; expose price tier in hotel response so agents can filter.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for free activities in Tokyo. Response returns general spots including Hamarikyu Gardens (fee=yes, price_band=low) as first result, which has an admission fee. Some free spots like Mount Takao (price_band=free) appear later. The server doesn't filter by free entry, diluting relevance significantly.
missing_data: Free-specific spots like Shinjuku Gyoen free days, free shrine grounds (Meiji, Asakusa approach), free observation decks, free parks like Yoyogi are not prominently surfaced.
wrong_data: Hamarikyu Gardens is listed but has fee=yes — directly contradicts the free-entry request.
improvement_hint: Add price_band filter parameter to get_spots; when query contains free/無料 intent signal, prioritize price_band=free results in ranking; surface fee=no and price_band=free spots prominently.

reasoning: Query is specifically about free-entry temples and shrines in Kamakura. search_hybrid retrieved results from Fukui, Kanagawa general, and a travel planning article — nothing specifically identifies free-entry Kamakura shrines/temples. Top result is a general travel planning page about temples elsewhere. Geographic focus on Kamakura is absent.
missing_data: Actual Kamakura-specific free temples (Engaku-ji grounds approach, Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, Sasuke Inari, Zeniarai Benzaiten) are absent. No price_band=free shrines from Kamakura municipality surfaced.
wrong_data: Second result is about a Fukui cat temple (御誕生寺) — completely off-topic geographically and thematically.
improvement_hint: Add prefecture/municipality scoping to search_hybrid; when query mentions specific city name, apply prefecture_code filter; improve BM25 to match 無料 + 鎌倉 joint tokens with municipality disambiguation.

reasoning: User wants cheap Fukuoka yatai (street food stalls) under ¥500/meal. get_local_food returns MAFF GI products (premium agricultural designations) and scraped content about tourist info — zero yatai/street food stall listings. The tool is fundamentally mismatched: GI products are not cheap food venues. No venue data at all.
missing_data: Fukuoka yatai locations, Nakasu yatai area, street food stall listings, affordable restaurant venues, Hakata ramen shops under ¥500 context are all absent.
wrong_data: Contains 'Cherry Blossoms in Fukuoka' named as a regional_dish entry — clearly a scrape contamination error where sightseeing content was ingested as food data.
improvement_hint: Add venue/restaurant category to local food tool; scrub scraped_local_food entries where description is clearly non-food content; add yatai as a searchable category; improve scrape extraction to separate food items from tourism information pages.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for free-admission shrines/temples in Kyoto. search_area returns 100 Kyoto shrine/temple entries but lacks any price/fee information — the agent cannot determine which are free. Returns obscure rural shrines (tier=broader) without descriptions or access info. No filtering for free entry is possible from this data.
missing_data: Price_band or fee field missing from search_area results; well-known free Kyoto shrines (Fushimi Inari, Heian Shrine grounds, Nijo Castle exterior) not surfaced with free-entry context.
improvement_hint: Add fee and price_band fields to search_area attraction results; when query includes 無料/免費 signal, surface spots with known free entry first; promote must_see tier shrines with free grounds over obscure broader-tier entries.

reasoning: Query asks for cheap eats in Sapporo — local markets and affordable restaurants under ¥1,500. get_local_food returns 500 MAFF GI products (Yubari Melon, dried sea cucumber, yams) — premium/agricultural designation items, not restaurants or market venues. No venue, no price, no proximity to Sapporo. Completely mismatched tool output.
missing_data: Nijo Market (二条市場) listings, Sapporo ramen venues, Curb Market (場外市場), affordable restaurant spots; market venue information is entirely absent.
improvement_hint: The local_food tool should include market venue data alongside product GI entries; add a venue_type field for market stalls; consider a separate get_markets tool or add market entries to get_spots with food-market kinds.

reasoning: User seeks hostel under ¥3,000/night in Osaka. get_hotels returns hotels without price data. Mix of hostel, hotel, luxury hotel (ANA Crowne Plaza, DoubleTree Hilton) — no filtering by price tier. Agent cannot identify budget options. Hostel lodging_type entries exist but no pricing to distinguish ¥3,000-level from luxury.
missing_data: Price band data for all entries; hostels and guesthouses need price_band field; capsule hotel category absent; no way for agent to filter to sub-¥3,000 options.
improvement_hint: Add price_band field to hotel/hostel records from OSM tags or scraped data; rank hostel/guesthouse/capsule_hotel lodging_types first when budget query detected; expose whether a property is budget tier.

reasoning: User asks where to buy cheap souvenirs — 100-yen shops and supermarkets. Query has no connection to Aichi; tool choice is get_local_specialty for Aichi food GI items (hatcho miso, winter melon, shiso flowers). Response is premium agricultural designations, not retail shopping venues. Intent completely missed: no 100-yen shop data, no supermarket, no souvenir shop.
missing_data: 100-yen shop locations, souvenir store venues, supermarkets; the entire retail shopping venue category is absent from the system.
improvement_hint: Add retail/shopping venue category to the data pipeline; consider 100-yen shop chains and local souvenir shops as valid tourism data; intent classifier should route souvenir queries to spot search with shopping kinds rather than specialty food.

reasoning: Query asks for free outdoor parks/nature in Japan (nation-wide). search_hybrid returns 10 results including some parks, but top result is a Yamaguchi city park tourism page (very generic), and one result (Nishiyama Park, Fukui) has 無料 explicitly. No price_band=free filter applied. Results are geographically scattered without free-entry confirmation for each.
missing_data: National parks (no entry fee), coastal parks, mountain hiking areas, riverside parks; free-entry confirmation for each result is absent; famous free parks like Yoyogi, Shinjuku Gyoen (specific days) not surfaced.
improvement_hint: Add price_band=free filter to search_hybrid; when no-fee intent detected, post-filter results to those with fee=no or price_band=free; expand national park coverage with explicit free-entry metadata.

reasoning: Korean query asks for free outdoor parks and spots in Tokyo. get_spots(Tokyo) returns rich data including Hamarikyu (fee=yes, price_band=low), Eitai Bridge (price_band=free), Mount Takao (price_band=free). However first result (Hamarikyu) has a fee — fails constraint. Free spots do appear but are not prioritized. Korean language name translations present.
missing_data: Free-specific top-tier Tokyo parks (Shinjuku Gyoen free days, Ueno Park, Yoyogi Park) not prioritized; no filtering to surface free-entry only; Hamarikyu prominently shown despite having fee.
improvement_hint: When price constraint (free/무료) detected, reorder results to surface price_band=free spots first; add fee=no filter parameter to get_spots.

reasoning: Arabic query asks for cheapest/free tourism spots in Nagano. get_spots returns good results including Kamikochi, Matsushiro Castle (fee=no, price_band=free), Tatsuoka Castle (price_band=low). Some genuinely free options visible. Tool correctly returns Nagano spots. Response is grounded and reasonably useful. Minor gap: no Arabic translation names.
missing_data: Arabic (ar) name translations absent; explicit free hiking trail data; price_band missing for several entries including Kamikochi (access has bus fee but trail free).
improvement_hint: Add Arabic to multilingual name coverage; surface price_band field more consistently; add explicit hiking trail free-entry indicators for mountain/nature spots.

reasoning: Luxury honeymoon in Hokkaido seeking high-end onsen ryokan. get_hotels returns hito_yu_kai members (authentic onsen ryokan) and ANA Holiday Inn. hito_yu_kai entries are genuine onsen ryokan with good provenance but lack luxury tier signals — no price range, no room type info. ANA Holiday Inn is not luxury. The response equips the agent with onsen ryokan data but cannot confirm luxury/high-end tier.
missing_data: Luxury signals (price tier, room amenities, kaiseki availability); top Hokkaido luxury onsen ryokan names like Yumoto Shiroganekan, Club Med Sahoro not present; coordinates missing on hito_yu_kai entries limiting usability.
improvement_hint: Add luxury_tier or star_rating field to hotel records; include coordinates for hito_yu_kai entries where available; add a filter for onsen_ryokan lodging_type to surface these first for honeymoon/luxury queries.

reasoning: Japanese query for luxury Kyoto ryokan with kaiseki and private open-air bath. Response returns the same hotel list as R-276: ABホテル (budget), APA GARDEN COURT (business), Comfort INN — no luxury ryokan at all. The hotel corpus for Kyoto lacks high-end ryokan like Tawaraya, Hiiragiya, or similar. Critical constraint failure for both price tier and lodging type.
missing_data: Luxury ryokan (高級旅館) entries for Kyoto completely absent; hito_yu_kai entries for Kyoto should appear; Tawaraya, Hiiragiya, and other famous Kyoto ryokan not in dataset.
improvement_hint: Expand Kyoto hotel coverage to include famous luxury ryokan; add hito_yu_kai or similar authoritative onsen/ryokan registry data for Kyoto; add luxury_tier filter so agents can identify appropriate options.

reasoning: Chinese query for luxury ocean-view resort in Okinawa, unlimited budget. Response starts with unnamed hostel and guesthouse (budget tier), then includes DoubleTree by Hilton and Hilton Okinawa Chatan Resort. Hilton properties are relevant but mixed with entirely inappropriate budget accommodations at top. No luxury resort specifics like Busena Terrace, ANA InterContinental.
missing_data: Top luxury Okinawa resorts (Busena Terrace, ANA InterContinental, Halekulani Okinawa) absent; ocean-view or beachfront filter unavailable; luxury tier flag missing.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_tier or star_rating to hotel records; rank Wikidata-sourced internationally recognized hotels higher for luxury queries; filter out unnamed hostel entries when query signals high-end preference.

reasoning: French query for luxury honeymoon in Kyoto+Nara. Tool called for Nara only. Returns hito_yu_kai onsen ryokan (good for Nara) and JW Marriott Nara (excellent luxury option), Hotel Nikko Nara — solid coverage for Nara luxury. Missing Kyoto entirely since only Nara was queried. JW Marriott is genuinely luxury. The Nara coverage is good; Kyoto coverage absent from this call.
missing_data: Kyoto luxury hotels not covered (only Nara queried); French name translations absent; no indication of kaiseki availability or romance amenities.
improvement_hint: When multi-city query detected, agents should call get_hotels for each prefecture; add French to multilingual hotel name coverage; surface hito_yu_kai designations prominently as quality signal.

reasoning: User asks for free/cheap sake tasting in Niigata. get_local_specialty returns 7 items: 4 sake brands/breweries with real Wikidata provenance (Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota) and 3 MAFF GI food items. Sake brand data is useful for identifying where to taste, but no brewery visiting/tasting information or pricing. Functional but thin for the specific tasting-venue aspect.
missing_data: Brewery visiting hours, tasting fee information, addresses for specific sake breweries (Ishimoto Shuzo, Asahi Shuzo); free tasting days or events not present.
improvement_hint: Add brewery visit/tasting metadata (opening_hours, fee, address) to sake_brewery entries; link sake brands to their physical brewery locations for visitor use.

reasoning: Korean query asks for cheap restaurants/markets in Sapporo under ¥1,000/meal. get_local_food(Hokkaido) returns 500 MAFF GI agricultural products (Yubari Melon, dried sea cucumber) — same issue as R-281. Korean translations present but the data is entirely the wrong type for the query: premium ingredient designations, not affordable dining venues.
missing_data: Sapporo ramen shop listings, Nijo Market (二条市場), Sapporo clock tower area food stalls, convenience store/depachika options; no venue data at all.
improvement_hint: Add food market venues to get_local_food results; create get_food_venues tool or extend get_spots to include dining/market kinds; MAFF GI results alone are insufficient for restaurant/market queries.

reasoning: Query about budget Yakushima — free hiking and cheap accommodation. get_spots returns Yakushima island (UNESCO, free hiking trails), Jomon Sugi with multilingual names, Yakushima Lighthouse with access details, and budget camping ground (屋久島青少年旅行村) with explicit fee info (¥440/night for adults). Excellent coverage for the specific query — real camping venue data with prices found.
missing_data: Trail access fees (Yakushima has environmental conservation fee for some trails); hostel/guesthouse listings missing from spots; mountain hut (山小屋) accommodation data absent.
improvement_hint: Add conservation fee warning for UNESCO World Heritage hiking areas; link camping spots to hotel data; add hostel/guesthouse entries for Yakushima town to complement the camping data.

reasoning: Vietnamese budget travel query: accommodation under ¥5,000/night and free spots in Kyoto. get_hotels returns identical Kyoto list to R-276/R-288 — no price data, includes luxury hotels (DoubleTree). Vietnamese (vi) language requested but no Vietnamese name translations present in results. Dual constraint (cheap hotel + free spots) only partially addressed and hotel price constraint entirely unmet.
missing_data: Vietnamese name translations for hotels; price_band data; hostels and guesthouses prominent in results; free attraction data not provided (would need second tool call).
improvement_hint: Add Vietnamese (vi) to hotel multilingual name coverage; add price_band to hotel records; surface hostel/guesthouse lodging_type entries first for budget queries; agent should also call get_spots with free-filter for free attraction component.

reasoning: Query asks for free/cheap museums in Tokyo. search_hybrid returns highly relevant results: International Museum Day free entry event (specific date, multiple museums listed with contact details), 東京農工大学科学博物館, 東京国立博物館, and 民音音楽博物館 (explicitly described as 無料 in Wikidata description). Query intent well-matched, results are actionable and specific with real venue names.
missing_data: Regular free-admission days for Tokyo National Museum; permanent free museums like Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building observation decks not surfaced.
improvement_hint: Add permanent fee=no flag to search results; surface permanent free-entry venues more prominently vs. one-time event free days; consider indexing observation decks as tourist attractions.

reasoning: Spanish query for free places in Nara on tight budget. get_spots returns excellent results: Mount Yoshino (outdoor/free hiking), Takamatsuzuka Tomb (price_band=free), Kofuku-ji (price_band=free, grounds free), Koriyama Castle. Free-entry data is present and surfaced. Multiple must_see tier results with price_band=free. Good coverage for the specific free-entry constraint.
missing_data: Nara Deer Park (free to enter) could be more explicitly surfaced; Spanish translations absent for some entries (name_en present); Kasuga Taisha free grounds not explicitly marked.
improvement_hint: Add Spanish to multilingual name coverage; ensure Nara Deer Park main area has explicit price_band=free; add explicit grounds_free vs. paid_interior distinction for temple complexes.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for exclusive Hakone ryokan with private open-air bath and kaiseki. get_hotels(Kanagawa) returns hostels (Aoki House, Kamejikan), Hilton Odawara, and UR apartment housing — no Hakone onsen ryokan at all. No hito_yu_kai entries for Kanagawa. The luxury ryokan segment in Hakone (Kanagawa's most famous onsen destination) is entirely absent.
missing_data: Hakone onsen ryokan entirely absent; famous Hakone ryokan (Gora Kadan, Kaiseki Yumoto Fujiya, Hakone Ginyu) not in dataset; hito_yu_kai entries for Kanagawa missing.
improvement_hint: Expand Kanagawa hotel data to include Hakone-area onsen ryokan; add hito_yu_kai data for Kanagawa prefecture; add municipality-level filtering for get_hotels so Hakone-specific results can be retrieved.

reasoning: German query for exclusive luxury onsen ryokan in Beppu. get_hotels(Oita) returns hito_yu_kai members (genuine onsen ryokan in Kyushu/Oita area, though some are in Kokonoe not Beppu city), plus AMANEK BEPPU YULA-RE (a real Beppu hotel). hito_yu_kai entries lack coordinates but are authentic. Mix of quality ryokan and guesthouses. Beppu coverage exists but luxury distinction and German translations absent.
missing_data: German (de) name translations absent; luxury/exclusive tier signals missing; famous Beppu establishments like Suginoi Hotel not present; coordinates for hito_yu_kai entries absent.
improvement_hint: Add German to multilingual hotel name coverage; add star_rating or luxury_tier field; ensure hito_yu_kai entries have coordinates where available; provide municipality subdivision (Beppu vs. Kokonoe) to distinguish results.

reasoning: Chinese query asks how to experience local food culture in Kyushu cheaply — recommends low-price restaurants or markets. get_local_food(Kumamoto) returns MAFF GI items (tatami igusa, Aso red cattle, burdock root) and truncated at 500/648. These are premium agricultural designations and craft materials, not cheap restaurant/market venues. Completely wrong data type for the intent.
missing_data: Kumamoto local food venues (horsemeat restaurants, karashirenkon shops, local markets); Kumamoto Castle area market information; affordable dining area guides.
wrong_data: First two results (igusa/tatami materials) are not food items at all — igusa is rush grass used for tatami mats, not edible.
improvement_hint: Filter non-food items from food tool output (tatami igusa is a craft material); add venue data to local food results; improve category classification to exclude agricultural materials from food queries.

reasoning: Query asks for free-admission Japanese cultural festivals. get_festivals(Aomori) returns 2 local festivals (Hachinohe Sansha Taisai, Aomori Nebuta) and multiple UNESCO ICH items. Aomori Nebuta is a famous free-to-watch street festival. Coverage is narrow (Aomori only, 2 local results). national_heritage section includes off-prefecture items (Dainichido Bugaku from Akita, Kawagoe Hikawa from Saitama). No admission price data for any festival.
missing_data: Free-entry confirmation for listed festivals; wider national coverage beyond Aomori; famous free festivals nationwide not retrieved; date/schedule for Nebuta (August) absent.
wrong_data: Dainichido Bugaku listed under Aomori national_heritage but is from Akita Prefecture — geographic mislabeling in scope field.
improvement_hint: Add admission_fee field to festival records; enable get_festivals nation-wide query without prefecture filter; fix geographic scoping so national_heritage section only includes items with clear prefectural connection; add event dates more consistently.
